name: Bert Slide (Pool)
history: A deep hand-drag carving slide in a bowl or pool, originating from Larry Bertlemann's surfing influence. One of the earliest identifiable named tricks in skateboarding.
youtube_query: bert slide pool bowl skateboard carving

name: Early Grab
history: Grabbing the board immediately at the beginning of the launch before reaching the peak of the air. A stylistic technique seen in old-school vert skating.

name: Judo Air
history: The front foot kicks out forward while grabbing the board heel-side with the back hand. Resembles a judo kick. A classic vert trick from the 1980s.
youtube_query: judo air skateboard vert trick tutorial

name: Iguana Air
history: A trick in which the back hand grabs the board while the legs are splayed out to the sides. The wide-legged position resembles an iguana. An old-school vert grab.

name: Roast Beef
history: The back hand grabs the heel edge of the board between the legs. A grab similar to the melon but with different arm positioning.

name: Cannonball Grab
history: Both knees are pulled up and both hands grab the board while in the air. Mimics a pool-diving cannonball position.

name: Tincan
history: Both hands grab the board near the rails while the legs are extended straight. A stylized variation of a double-grab.

name: Spaghetti Legs
history: A loose, wobbly leg extension during a grab, where the legs are left dangling. More of a style variation than a specific trick.

name: Kickflip to Tail Stall
history: A kickflip that lands in a tailstall on the ramp coping. Combines the flat-ground kickflip with a vert lip trick.
youtube_query: kickflip tail stall vert skateboard tutorial

name: Heelflip to Rock n Roll
history: A heelflip on the ramp that lands in a rock-and-roll position on the coping. Mixes street-style flip tricks with vert lip tricks.
youtube_query: heelflip rock and roll vert skateboard

name: 5-0 to Kickflip Out (Vert)
history: A 5-0 grind on the vert coping that exits with a kickflip. A modern tech vert trick blending grinding and flip tricks.

name: Channel Gap
history: An ollie or trick over a gap between two ramps or sections of a skatepark. The channel gap format is a classic skatepark design and trick challenge.

name: Step-Up
history: Jumping up from a lower level to a higher one using an ollie. The step-up is the reverse challenge of a step-down or stair set.
youtube_query: ollie step up skateboard trick tutorial

name: Step-Down
history: Rolling or jumping from a higher surface to a lower one. Step-downs allow for speed and larger tricks due to the extra airtime.

name: Beni Grind
history: A 5-0 grind variation on vert where the board is tilted at an extreme angle. Named for a specific style developed in bowl and vert skating.
youtube_query: beni grind vert skateboard bowl trick

name: Three Sixty Spin
history: A full 360-degree body spin in the air on a vert ramp, without the board flipping. A crowd-pleasing spin trick.

name: Bean Plant
history: The front foot is placed on the coping while the back foot is still on the board. An old-school plant trick from vert skating.
youtube_query: bean plant skateboard vert trick tutorial

name: Airborne 360 Grab
history: A 360-degree spin combined with a grab while fully airborne on a vert ramp. A combination of rotation and style.

name: Handstand Fingerflip
history: A handstand on the board from which the board is then finger-flipped. An extremely advanced freestyle combination of gymnastics and board tricks.

name: Nollie Flip to Nosegrind
history: A nollie kickflip that lands in a nosegrind. A creative and technical combination of a flip entry with the nose lock.

name: Switch Heelflip Noseslide
history: A switch heelflip into a noseslide. Combining the difficulty of switch and heelflip with the precision of the noseslide.

name: Switch Kickflip Noseslide
history: A switch kickflip into a noseslide. A very advanced tech street trick.

name: Tré Flip to Tailslide
history: A 360 flip (tre flip) into a tailslide. One of the most demanding ledge trick combinations.

name: Inward Heel Noseslide
history: An inward heelflip into a noseslide. Among the rarest and most technical flip-to-slide combinations.

name: Bigspin to Boardslide
history: A bigspin (360 body + 360 board) into a boardslide. The full combination spin landing into a perpendicular slide.

name: Underflip
history: The board is flipped from underneath using the toes or sole of the foot. Related to the hospital flip but initiated from below the board.

name: Footwork Combo
history: A rapid series of footwork maneuvers on the board without an ollie, often performed to music. A hallmark of competitive freestyle skateboarding.

name: Pogo Hop
history: Multiple consecutive pogo (tail bounce) hops. A show-stopping freestyle sequence performed on the tail of the board.

name: Fakie Blunt to Forward
history: A fakie blunt stall on coping that exits rolling forward (not fakie). Requires the skater to pivot through the stall.
youtube_query: fakie blunt to forward skateboard ramp

name: Switch Rock to Fakie
history: A rock to fakie performed in switch stance. The body mechanics are reversed while the front truck still needs to rock on the coping.
youtube_query: switch rock to fakie skateboard ramp tutorial

name: Nollie Rock to Fakie
history: A rock to fakie from nollie stance. The nose pops to initiate the rock before pulling back to fakie.
youtube_query: nollie rock to fakie skateboard ramp

name: Kickflip Disaster
history: A kickflip combined with a disaster (180 rotation to land centered on coping). The board flips as it rotates onto the coping.

name: Heelflip Disaster
history: A heelflip combined with a disaster on a ramp. An advanced ramp trick combining flip and coping technique.

name: Revert
history: A 180-degree pivot performed while rolling, without leaving the ground. Used to change direction after tricks or as part of a combo.

name: Kickflip Revert
history: A kickflip followed immediately by a 180-degree revert to land fakie. Combines the flip trick with the revert in one smooth motion.

name: Heelflip Revert
history: A heelflip followed by a revert to land fakie. A smooth transition from a flip trick into a fakie stance.

name: 180 Nosegrind Revert
history: A nosegrind entered with a 180 that exits with a revert to regular. Combining the spin entry and revert exit creates a full circular flow.

name: Tailslide Revert
history: A tailslide that exits with a 180-degree revert. Changing from the slide's direction into a backward exit.

name: Boardslide Revert
history: A boardslide that exits with a revert to fakie. A stylish way to exit a boardslide by spinning the body.

name: Shoveit Revert
history: A pop shove-it followed by a revert. The board rotates backside 180 and then the body rotates to land fakie.

name: Varial Flip Revert
history: A varial kickflip followed by a revert to land fakie. Combines the shove-it kickflip with a landing pivot.

name: Fake Heel
history: A fakie heelflip exiting to fakie (no rotation). One of the standard fakie flip tricks in modern skating.

name: Nollie Flip Nosegrind
history: A nollie kickflip into a nosegrind. A creative ledge combination using the nollie flip's momentum to lock into the grind.

name: Kinked Nosegrind
history: A nosegrind on a kinked (angled) rail. The change in angle during the grind requires body adjustment and is a benchmark street trick.

name: Backside Kickflip Nosegrind
history: A backside 180 kickflip into a nosegrind. The spin sets up the grind approach from a creative direction.

name: Switch Smith to Fakie
history: A switch Smith grind that exits rolling fakie. Exits the grind by allowing the board to roll backward.
youtube_query: switch smith to fakie skateboard trick

name: Nose Grab (Flatground)
history: Grabbing the nose of the board while performing an ollie or other aerial trick. Shows height and control.
youtube_query: nose grab ollie flatground skateboard tutorial

name: Tail Grab (Flatground)
history: Grabbing the tail of the board during a flatground ollie. A classic style trick on flat or down gaps.
youtube_query: tail grab ollie flatground skateboard tutorial

name: Indy Grab (Flatground)
history: An indy grab performed on a flatground ollie. Used to show style and control in the air.
youtube_query: indy grab flatground ollie skateboard tutorial

name: Melon Grab (Flatground)
history: A melon grab performed during a flatground ollie. A clean grab demonstrating height and technique.
youtube_query: melon grab flatground ollie skateboard tutorial

name: Mute Grab (Flatground)
history: A mute grab on a flatground ollie. One of the most common grab variations used to add style to basic ollies.
youtube_query: mute grab flatground ollie skateboard tutorial
